title: Debt-to-Income (DTI) Ratio Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate your debt-to-income ratio and see how it compares to standard lending thresholds, instantly in your browser.
shortDescription: Calculate your DTI ratio from monthly debt payments and gross income.

guideSections.heading: Why Lenders Care About This Ratio More Than Credit Score Alone
guideSections.body: Credit score reflects how reliably debt has been repaid historically, while DTI reflects whether there's enough income capacity to take on more debt right now. A strong credit history with a high DTI can still result in loan denial, since the lender's primary concern at that point is monthly cash flow, not past behavior.

guideSections.heading: Why 'Gross' Income, Not Take-Home Pay, Is the Standard
guideSections.body: DTI calculations conventionally use gross monthly income before taxes and deductions, which produces a different — usually more favorable — ratio than calculating against take-home pay. Using net income instead of gross when comparing your DTI against published lending thresholds will make the comparison inaccurate.
